nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 南美鸛
nodes.summary: 南美鸛（Milky stork，学名 Mycteria cinerea），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，已关联9个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括海岸湿地、河口、沼泽，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、非法猎捕、森林砍伐等5项。

nodes.name: 泰国
nodes.summary: 泰国是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联251种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、銹紅林鷓鴣、环颈山鹧鸪等251种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 越南
nodes.summary: 越南是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联181种鸟类，包括青头潜鸭、花脸鸭、黑脸琵鹭等181种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 爪哇岛
nodes.summary: 爪哇岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联60种鸟类，包括绿孔雀、Moustached hawk-cuckoo、八声杜鹃等60种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 蘇門答臘島
nodes.summary: 蘇門答臘島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联94种鸟类，包括罗氏山鹧鸪、蘇門答臘山鷓鴣、長嘴山鶉等94种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 苏拉威西岛
nodes.summary: 苏拉威西岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联31种鸟类，包括菲律宾冢雉、蓝胸鹑、白喉林鸽等31种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 柬埔寨
nodes.summary: 柬埔寨是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联93种鸟类，包括栗头山鹧鸪、橙颈山鹧鸪、绿孔雀等93种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 霹靂州
nodes.summary: 霹靂州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括南美鸛，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 海岸湿地
nodes.summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

nodes.name: 斯坦福·莱佛士
nodes.summary: 斯坦福·莱佛士是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联5种鸟类，包括Malayan crested fireback、山皇鸠、黑枕燕鸥等5种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 马来西亚
nodes.summary: 马来西亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联156种鸟类，包括马来冠眼斑雉、婆羅洲地杜鵑、Short-toed coucal等156种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 污染
nodes.summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

nodes.name: 森林砍伐
nodes.summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 沼泽
nodes.summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

nodes.name: 河口
nodes.summary: 淡水与海水交汇形成的高生产力边缘生态系统。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The milky stork (Mycteria cinerea) is a stork species inhabiting predominantly mangroves in Southeast Asia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|Milky stork in Borneo The milky stork's range is restricted to Southeast Asia, where it is widely but patchily distributed. It occurs in Sumatra (its global stronghold), Java, Sulawesi, eastern Malaysia, Cambodia, southern Vietnam (where it probably largely recolonised after the war of 1963–75), Bali, Sumbawa, Lombok and Buton. It historically occurred in southern Thailand,Byers O, Brouwer K, Coulter M, Seal US

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: For example, it was formerly widely distributed along the coasts of the Malaysian Peninsula, but is now restricted to Matang Mangrove Forest in Perak. The milky stork is predominantly a lowland coastal species throughout its range; where it inhabits mangrove, freshwater and peat swamps, and estuaries. The only proven breeding records however are reported from mangroves bordering the feeding grounds.Danielsen F, Skov H

links.label: 栖息于

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Since the 1980's, the global milky stork population has declined from 5,000 to 2,000 individuals due to habitat destruction, overfishing and illegal smuggling of chicks

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The global milky stork population has been declining substantially, especially from the late 1980s, and has been largely attributable to habitat destruction and disturbance through mangrove deforestation for human activities such as fish farming, tidal rice cultivation, timber exploitation, and in Indonesia, human resettlement. Deforestation has led to a lack of suitable mature trees for the species to nest in and thereby affected breeding success. Breeding colonies have also declined through increased illegal international trade in this bird from the mid-1980s and, especially in South Sumatra, poaching of eggs and juveniles for human consumption. Juveniles in particular have been sold to and purchased by zoos in Singapore, Kuala Lumpur, Brunei, and several European zoos. The milky stork is said to be generally vulnerable to human disturbance, which may also explain the marked widespread decline of this species

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Fakultas Kehutanan IPB/Disney Wildlife Conservation/Wildlife Trust. Another potential threat to the milky stork is contamination of its natural habitat with elevated concentrations of ions of metals such as copper, zinc and lead
